summary: Compute the Euclidean distance between two 2D points using distancePoints.
Compute the absolute area of a polygon via the shoelace formula using polygonAreaShoelace; fewer than 3 points yields 0.
Test whether a 2D point lies inside a polygon using ray casting with pointInPolygon; uses the half-open crossing rule so points exactly on certain edges may return true.

tests.desc: left edge counted inside by half-open rule

summary: Determine whether two 2D line segments intersect using segmentsIntersect, returning true for both proper crossings and touching or collinear-overlapping cases.
Compute the area of the convex hull of 2D points using convexHullArea, building the hull with the Andrew monotone chain algorithm then applying the shoelace formula; fewer than 3 points yields 0.
Compute the L1 Manhattan distance between two 2D points using manhattanDistance.
Compute the axis-aligned bounding box of 2D points using boundingBox, returning [minX, minY, maxX, maxY]; an empty input yields [0,0,0,0].
Rotate a 2D point around a center by an angle in degrees counterclockwise using rotatePointDeg, rounding each coordinate to 6 decimals.
